title: Neoachmandra boholensis
intro: Neoachmandra boholensis, also known as "bohol achmandra," is a type of plant in the Achariaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Neoachmandra boholensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neoachmandra boholensis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Neoachmandra boholensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. It belongs to the genus Neoachmandra. The species is endemic to the Philippines. More specifically, it is found in the island of Bohol. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Neoachmandra boholensis has been found to possess various medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its uses.
